what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies the ambient 'react' commentary channel is silent in listening, focus, and off modes, and that switching to watch-and-react opens the channel without invoking a model.
observations: Uses a temporary FsStore and discovers nodes from ROOT/nodes.
Creates a 'react' node group with a constant node that emits an event.
Checks suite.react(g)['comment'] is empty in listening, focus, and off modes.
In watch-and-react mode only checks that suite.get_mode() returns 'watch-and-react'.
Does not call or stub an LLM; the test is purely about the mode gate.
Tallies passing checks with a global PASS counter and a custom check() helper.
Cleans up the temporary store directory in a finally block.
standouts: Tests absence of behavior (silent output) rather than correct output.
Mode-gating is verified offline with no model invocation.
Watch-and-react mode is accepted as the single valid trigger.
conventions: Self-contained test script run as __main__ with sys.path insertion.
Uses tempfile for an isolated store directory.
Uses an assertion helper that labels checks and counts passes.
Discovers nodes from a sibling 'nodes' directory.
concerns: Only validates the mode string in watch-and-react, not that a comment is actually produced.
Global mutable PASS counter is shared across the module.
The docstring mentions proving the live comment by use, but no model call occurs.
notes: A mode-gate acceptance test that makes sure ambient react commentary stays silent unless the system is explicitly in watch-and-react mode.
questions: What is the exact shape and semantics of suite.react(g)?
How is watch-and-react mode implemented and how does it decide whether to call a model?
What is the RHM and how does it generate comments?
What event does the constant node emit and how is it observed by the react channel?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches: it proves the mode gate is closed outside watch-and-react and that watch-and-react is accepted, but it does not actually prove a live comment is generated.
apparent_themes: Offline acceptance testing of LLM-gated features
intention_signals: The file is trying to guarantee that ambient reaction commentary is strictly gated to watch-and-react mode so it cannot fire or incur model cost in other modes, while confirming that the intended mode is recognized.
novelty: It treats 'silent in the wrong modes' as a first-class acceptance property and avoids any model invocation while still testing a model-gated feature.
contribution: A documented, runnable acceptance check that guards the mode gate for ambient react commentary and records the expected silence of the feature outside watch-and-react mode.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that verifies the ambient react commentary channel is silent in listening, focus, and off modes and that watch-and-react is the valid mode that opens the channel.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to prove offline that ambient reaction commentary is mode-gated to watch-and-react and silent everywhere else.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition, increments the global pass count, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a minimal local assertion helper used throughout the test script. It asserts that cond is true with a labeled failure message, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints a concise ok line for each passing check.
